title: Capsella nylandica
taxonomy: Capsella nylandica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Capsella, which is a group of small, annual weeds. The plant is also known as the shepherds' cress or yellow rocket. Its classification is closely related to other plants in the Brassicaceae family, such as Arabidopsis and Cardamine. Capsella nylandica is often found growing in a variety of environments, from fields to woodland edges.

intro:
There is limited information on Capsella nylandica. However, if you are referring to the plant Capsella bursa-pastoris, I can provide the following information:

Capsella bursa-pastoris, commonly known as shepherd's purse, is a biennial or annual plant in the Brassicaceae family that is often found growing in fields and along roadsides. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and relieving coughs. The bioactive constituents of Capsella bursa-pastoris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glucosinolates, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with shepherd's purse include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and support wound healing.
